title: Annotations & text: arrows, shapes, highlights, text blocks
goal: Best-in-class screenshot annotation: pick tool, drag, done — with delightful defaults.

tasks.title: Tool state machine in AppState (select/crop/arrow/rect/ellipse/highlight/text)
tasks.notes: Tool enum + smart-default content live in PhotonzCore/Tools.swift (tested). AppState.activeTool; setTool clears marquee + layer selection so select-mode chrome never shows under a drawing tool. Annotation tools are sticky (each drag adds a layer); Esc with nothing in flight reverts to select via onToolChange. Toolbar: V/A/L/R/O/H single-key shortcuts, active tool = accent-filled circle; crop/text/zoom-callout are disabled placeholders (phases 4/3.4/5). Opening a new document resets to select.

tasks.title: Drag-to-create annotation layers wired to rasterizers from 1.4
tasks.notes: Core: AnnotationDrag (⇧ = 45° angle snap for line/arrow, square for rect/ellipse/highlight; view-space click tolerance) + AnnotationBuilder (frame = drag bbox padded by AnnotationContent.renderPadding so round caps and arrowhead wings never clip; Geometry.arrowheadHalfWidth is shared with the rasterizer so padding can't drift). Canvas previews the in-flight drag as CAShapeLayers in view coords (stroked shaft + fill-only arrowhead sublayer, multiply filter for highlight); after commit the preview is HELD until a different composite CGImage arrives (identity check) so the annotation never flashes out during the async re-render. Click-sized drags commit nothing. Verified headlessly: ad-hoc harness compiling CanvasView.swift against built modules, synthesized NSEvent drags, pixel asserts + PNG review (32 checks: preview fidelity, commit coords, Esc cancel, sticky tool, marquee regression). GOTCHA for 3.5: resizing an annotation layer's frame does not remap content start/end yet — handle-resize of an annotation distorts/clips; 3.5 must scale endpoints with the frame.

tasks.title: Style popover: color swatches, stroke width, glass styling
tasks.notes: Core: AnnotationStyles (PhotonzCore/AnnotationStyles.swift, tested) — shared stroke color for arrow/line/rect/ellipse, separate highlight color, strokeWidth applies to stroke shapes only (Tool.usesStrokeWidth). Tool.defaultAnnotation now delegates to AnnotationStyles() so defaults can't drift. AppState persists styles to UserDefaults key 'annotationStyles'. UI: toolbar swatch button (visible when annotation tool active, S toggles popover), glass popover (presentationBackground(.clear)+glassEffect — system bezel shows as a light halo, phase-7 polish candidate; unselected width dots are low-contrast on glass). CanvasView drag preview takes annotationContent from AppState so previews match the picked style. Verified with in-process harness (/tmp/photonz-style-harness): real EditorView + NSEvents, 31 checks + real-screen glass capture. MANUAL CHECK PENDING: Esc with popover open should close only the popover, not also reset the tool (harness couldn't verify real key routing). 3.5 should reuse this popover for editing a selected annotation's style.

tasks.title: Text blocks: click to place, inline editing, font picker (font/size/weight/color)
tasks.notes: Core: TextWeight enum + TextContent.weight (custom decode defaults legacy payloads to regular), TextStyles (font/size/weight/color, persisted to UserDefaults 'textStyles'), TextBuilder frame math. Render: TextRasterizer.naturalSize (frameInset slack so glyphs never clip; do NOT inset the draw path — CoreText drops lines in frames a hair shorter than line height) and weight-aware font(for:) that enumerates the family's upright faces and picks the nearest weight (a weight trait in a descriptor alone does nothing). UI: text tool (T), click places an inline NSTextView styled via the rasterizer's face at fontSize*zoom; click-away commits (empty draft = no layer; emptied re-edit deletes the layer), Esc cancels, double-click re-edits (checked BEFORE resize handles — on small text layers handle hit zones cover the frame and would eat the double-click). AppState.editingTextLayerID hides the layer in submit() while the editor overlays it. Editor wrap width runs to the canvas right edge and commit re-measures with the same width. Verified: 47-check in-process harness (/tmp/photonz-text-harness, same pattern as 3.3) + PNGs. GOTCHA for 3.5: resizing a text layer's frame re-wraps/rescales text at render time (rasterized at frame size); handle-resize semantics for text need a decision in 3.5. Polish candidates (phase 7): popover menu labels low-contrast on glass; editor border box spans to canvas right edge even for short text.

tasks.title: Editable after the fact: select annotation → adjust endpoints/style
tasks.notes: Core (PhotonzCore/AnnotationEditing.swift, tested): AnnotationBuilder.updating (rebuild layer between doc-space endpoints, identity/style preserved, frame re-padded like a fresh drag), .resized (handle-resize remaps endpoints proportionally — closes the 3.2 distort/clip gotcha; Layer.resized(to:) dispatches by content), .restyled (color/strokeWidth; width changes re-pad frame around fixed doc endpoints), AnnotationEndpointDrag (⇧ 45° snap around the fixed end), AnnotationEndpoints.hit (8 screen-pt tolerance), Layer.contains/Document.hitTest now zoom-aware: line/arrow hit within strokeWidth/2 + 6/zoom of the segment (empty bbox corners fall through). DECISIONS: text layers never frame-resize (allowsFrameResize=false; size via font picker, the render-time re-wrap was unpredictable); line/arrow show 2 round endpoint handles, no frame outline; restyling a selected annotation also updates the new-annotation defaults. UI: endpoint drags reuse the CAShapeLayer vector preview over the drag-preview underlay (zero per-move rendering; endpointHoldLayerID keeps underlay+preview up until the post-commit composite lands); style popover targets the selection via AppState.selectedAnnotationLayer. FIXED BUG: click-select leaves dragPreview alive, so content edits (restyle/undo/redo) under it showed a stale sprite — AppState.discardDragPreview() now drops it. KNOWN APPROXIMATION: box-annotation handle-resize stretches the sprite mid-drag (stroke appears scaled) and snaps to constant stroke on commit. Verified: 50-check harness /tmp/photonz-35-harness (same compile pattern: app sources minus PhotonzApp.swift + main.swift against module .o files), PNGs reviewed. Harness gotcha: keyboard shortcuts typed while the inline text editor is focused go INTO the editor — commit drafts via click-away first.

tasks.title: Smart defaults: red arrows, yellow highlight, auto-contrast text shadow
tasks.notes: Partially shipped with 3.1: Tool.defaultAnnotation gives red (#FF3B30) arrows/lines/rects/ellipses and yellow (#FFD60A) highlight. Remaining: auto-contrast text shadow (depends on 3.4).
